rationale: The "satisfying organization" 3D-render hook lives in a totally different visual world from Block Blast — pastel kitchen tones vs. saturated blocks on a navy grid — and there's no natural bridge from pouring spices into the drag-and-drop line-clear loop without a heavy-handed "blocks pour into grid" reframe. Audience has partial overlap (the same 25-55 casual scrollers enjoy ASMR-organization content), but with velocity 0.21 and a single advertiser behind it, this archetype isn't worth a bespoke production unless we test a low-cost adaptation that literally translates the "pour into container" satisfaction beat into blocks cascading to clear a near-full board. Pass on a faithful clone; only greenlight if creative can hard-marry the satisfaction trigger to the line-clear payoff in the first 2 seconds.
